title: Advanced Usage
text: table includes built-right-click context menu (insert row / , remove row, undo / redo, copy, clear selection, clear column).

title: Advanced Usage
text: Compare row counts edit. helper logic observeEvent() call:
code: classify_row_change <- function(old_count, new_count) {   if (new_count > old_count) "added"   else if (new_count < old_count) "removed"   else "unchanged" }  classify_row_change(5, 6) #> [1] "added" classify_row_change(5, 4) #> [1] "removed" classify_row_change(5, 5) #> [1] "unchanged" server <- function(input, output, session) {   row_count <- reactiveVal(5)    observeEvent(input$my_table_edited, {     data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(input$my_table_edited)     change <- classify_row_change(row_count(), nrow(data))     if (change == "added")   showNotification("Row added")     if (change == "removed") showNotification("Row removed")     row_count(nrow(data))   }) }

title: Advanced Usage
text: Define pure validator function can run vignette call shiny observeEvent():
code: validate_data <- function(data) {   errors <- character()    if (any(is.na(data$name) | data$name == "")) {     errors <- c(errors, "Name required")   }    if (any(data$quantity < 0, na.rm = TRUE)) {     errors <- c(errors, "Quantity must be >= 0")   }    errors }  # A clean dataset returns no errors clean <- data.frame(   name = c("A", "B"),   quantity = c(1, 2),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) validate_data(clean) #> character(0)  # A dirty dataset reports both rules dirty <- data.frame(   name = c("", "B"),   quantity = c(-1, 2),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) validate_data(dirty) #> [1] "Name required"         "Quantity must be >= 0" server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$my_table_edited, {     data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(input$my_table_edited)     errors <- validate_data(data)      if (length(errors) > 0) {       showNotification(         paste("Errors:", paste(errors, collapse = ", ")),         type = "warning"       )     }   }) }

title: Advanced Usage
text: small dataset demonstrate CSV / JSON export: logic plugged downloadHandler():
code: table_data <- data.frame(   product = c("Widget", "Gadget"),   price = c(29.99, 49.99),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  csv_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".csv") write.csv(table_data, csv_path, row.names = FALSE) file.exists(csv_path) #> [1] TRUE  json_path <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") jsonlite::write_json(table_data, json_path, pretty = TRUE) readLines(json_path, n = 3) #> [1] "["                            "  {"                          #> [3] "    \"product\": \"Widget\"," ui <- fluidPage(   esq_tableInput("data_table", data = table_data,                  columns = list(list(name = "product", type = "text"),                                 list(name = "price", type = "numeric"))),   downloadButton("download", "Download CSV") )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   current_data <- reactiveVal(table_data)    observeEvent(input$data_table_edited, {     current_data(jsonlite::fromJSON(input$data_table_edited))   })    output$download <- downloadHandler(     filename = function() paste0("data_", Sys.Date(), ".csv"),     content = function(file) write.csv(current_data(), file, row.names = FALSE)   )    output$download_json <- downloadHandler(     filename = function() paste0("data_", Sys.Date(), ".json"),     content = function(file) jsonlite::write_json(current_data(), file, pretty = TRUE)   ) }

title: Advanced Usage
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   auto_save <- debounce(reactive(input$my_table_edited), 2000)    observe({     data_json <- auto_save()     req(data_json)      data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(data_json)     saveRDS(data, "auto_save.rds")      showNotification("Auto-saved", duration = 1)   }) }

title: Advanced Usage
code: library(plotly)  ui <- fluidPage(   fluidRow(     column(6, esq_tableInput("data", data = initial_data, columns = cols)),     column(6, plotlyOutput("chart"))   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   table_data <- reactive({     if (!is.null(input$data_edited)) {       jsonlite::fromJSON(input$data_edited)     } else {       initial_data     }   })    output$chart <- renderPlotly({     plot_ly(table_data(), x = ~category, y = ~value, type = "bar")   }) }

title: Advanced Usage
text: Build styling chunk htmltools can inspected dropped shiny tags$head():
code: custom_css <- htmltools::tags$style(htmltools::HTML("   .esq-handsontable th {     background: #f5f5f5;     font-weight: 600;   }   .esq-handsontable td.htDimmed {     background: #fafafa;     color: #999;   }   .esq-handsontable td.htInvalid {     background: #ffebee !important;   } "))  inherits(custom_css, "shiny.tag") #> [1] TRUE ui <- fluidPage(   tags$head(custom_css),   esq_tableInput("styled_table", data = my_data, columns = my_columns) )

title: Advanced Usage
text: Built-Handsontable shortcuts:

title: Advanced Usage
text: safe parser returns NULL bad JSON:
code: safe_parse <- function(json) {   tryCatch(     jsonlite::fromJSON(json),     error = function(e) NULL   ) }  safe_parse('[{"a": 1}]') #>   a #> 1 1 safe_parse("not valid json") #> NULL observeEvent(input$my_table_edited, {   data <- safe_parse(input$my_table_edited)   if (is.null(data)) {     showNotification("Error parsing data", type = "error")     return()   }   # Process data... })

title: Advanced Usage
text: Table rendering? Check package installation Verify data frame structure Dropdowns empty? Ensure source character vector Check typos column names Updates working? Verify inputId matches Check session passed correctly Performance issues? Reduce row count (use pagination) Use simpler column types Minimize columns

title: Column Types and Configuration
text: column configured list various options:

title: Column Types and Configuration
text: Standard text input fields:
code: description_col <- list(name = "description", type = "text", width = 200)  # Read-only text id_col <- list(name = "id", type = "text", readOnly = TRUE, width = 80)  str(description_col) #> List of 3 #>  $ name : chr "description" #>  $ type : chr "text" #>  $ width: num 200

title: Column Types and Configuration
text: Number input automatic validation:
code: quantity_col <- list(name = "quantity", type = "numeric", width = 100) price_col <- list(name = "price", type = "numeric", width = 80)  str(price_col) #> List of 3 #>  $ name : chr "price" #>  $ type : chr "numeric" #>  $ width: num 80

title: Column Types and Configuration
text: Boolean checkbox cells. Data contain TRUE/FALSE values.
code: active_col <- list(name = "active", type = "checkbox", width = 70) str(active_col) #> List of 3 #>  $ name : chr "active" #>  $ type : chr "checkbox" #>  $ width: num 70

title: Column Types and Configuration
text: Single-select dropdown menus:
code: category_col <- list(   name = "category",   type = "dropdown",   source = c("Electronics", "Clothing", "Food", "Other"),   width = 120 )  # Allow empty values optional_col <- list(   name = "optional",   type = "dropdown",   source = c("", "Option A", "Option B"),   width = 100 )  # Disable validation flexible_col <- list(   name = "flexible",   type = "dropdown",   source = c("A", "B", "C"),   validate = FALSE )  category_col$source #> [1] "Electronics" "Clothing"    "Food"        "Other"

title: Column Types and Configuration
text: Select multiple values list. Multi-select values stored comma-separated strings (e.g., "tag1, tag2, tag3").
code: # Basic multi-select tags_col <- list(   name = "tags",   type = "multiselect",   source = c("urgent", "review", "approved", "pending"),   width = 180 )  # Sortable multi-select (drag to reorder) priorities_col <- list(   name = "priorities",   type = "multiselect",   source = c("Critical", "High", "Medium", "Low"),   sortable = TRUE,   width = 180 )  priorities_col$sortable #> [1] TRUE

title: Column Types and Configuration
text: Add helpful tooltips column headers:
code: my_data <- data.frame(   id = c("A1", "A2"),   name = c("First", "Second"),   status = c("open", "closed"),   priority = c("High", "Low"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  my_columns <- list(   list(name = "id", type = "text", readOnly = TRUE),   list(name = "name", type = "text"),   list(name = "status", type = "dropdown", source = c("open", "closed")),   list(name = "priority", type = "dropdown",        source = c("High", "Medium", "Low")) )  tooltip_ui <- esq_tableInput(   "my_table",   data = my_data,   columns = my_columns,   column_descriptions = list(     id = "Unique identifier (auto-generated)",     name = "Full name of the item",     status = "Current processing status",     priority = "Task priority level (drag to reorder)"   ) )  inherits(tooltip_ui, "shiny.tag") #> [1] FALSE

title: Column Types and Configuration
text: Wrap shiny app view interactively:
code: inventory <- data.frame(   sku = c("SKU-001", "SKU-002"),   name = c("Widget", "Gadget"),   category = c("Electronics", "Tools"),   price = c(29.99, 49.99),   tags = c("sale, featured", "new"),   in_stock = c(TRUE, FALSE),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  inventory_columns <- list(   list(name = "sku", type = "text", width = 90, readOnly = TRUE),   list(name = "name", type = "text", width = 150),   list(name = "category", type = "dropdown",        source = c("Electronics", "Tools", "Clothing"), width = 110),   list(name = "price", type = "numeric", width = 80),   list(name = "tags", type = "multiselect",        source = c("sale", "featured", "new", "clearance"),        sortable = TRUE, width = 150),   list(name = "in_stock", type = "checkbox", width = 70) )  inventory_ui <- esq_tableInput(   "inventory",   data = inventory,   columns = inventory_columns,   column_descriptions = list(     sku = "Stock Keeping Unit (read-only)",     name = "Product name",     category = "Product category",     price = "Price in USD",     tags = "Product tags (drag to prioritize)",     in_stock = "Currently in stock?"   ) )  inventory #>       sku   name    category price           tags in_stock #> 1 SKU-001 Widget Electronics 29.99 sale, featured     TRUE #> 2 SKU-002 Gadget       Tools 49.99            new    FALSE ui <- fluidPage(inventory_ui)  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$inventory_edited, {     data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(input$inventory_edited)     print(data)   }) }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Conditional Cell Properties
text: cell_conditions parameter allows dynamically change cell properties based cell values. enables context-aware forms fields become relevant irrelevant based user selections.

title: Conditional Cell Properties
code: example_condition <- list(   list(     column = "target_column",       # Column to modify     when_column = "trigger_column", # Column to watch     when_value = "trigger_value",   # Value that triggers condition     readOnly = TRUE                 # Property to set   ) )  str(example_condition) #> List of 1 #>  $ :List of 4 #>   ..$ column     : chr "target_column" #>   ..$ when_column: chr "trigger_column" #>   ..$ when_value : chr "trigger_value" #>   ..$ readOnly   : logi TRUE

title: Conditional Cell Properties
text: Disable fields based data type selection:
code: analysis_data <- data.frame(   name = c("Analysis 1", "Analysis 2"),   dataType = c("Simulated", "Observed"),   scenario = c("Baseline", ""),   dataset = c("", "Clinical Data"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  analysis_columns <- list(   list(name = "name", type = "text"),   list(name = "dataType", type = "dropdown",        source = c("Simulated", "Observed")),   list(name = "scenario", type = "dropdown",        source = c("Baseline", "Treatment A", "Treatment B")),   list(name = "dataset", type = "dropdown",        source = c("Clinical Data", "Lab Data")) )  analysis_conditions <- list(   # Disable scenario when Observed   list(     column = "scenario",     when_column = "dataType",     when_value = "Observed",     readOnly = TRUE   ),   # Disable dataset when Simulated   list(     column = "dataset",     when_column = "dataType",     when_value = "Simulated",     readOnly = TRUE   ) )  analysis_ui <- esq_tableInput(   "analysis",   data = analysis_data,   columns = analysis_columns,   cell_conditions = analysis_conditions )  inherits(analysis_ui, "shiny.tag") #> [1] FALSE

title: Conditional Cell Properties
code: payment_conditions <- list(   # Disable amount for Free   list(     column = "amount",     when_column = "payment",     when_value = "Free",     readOnly = TRUE   ),   # Also disable amount for Trial   list(     column = "amount",     when_column = "payment",     when_value = "Trial",     readOnly = TRUE   ) )  length(payment_conditions) #> [1] 2

title: Conditional Cell Properties
text: Disable multiple columns based one trigger:
code: shipping_conditions <- list(   # If shipping is "Pickup", disable these fields:   list(     column = "address",     when_column = "shipping",     when_value = "Pickup",     readOnly = TRUE   ),   list(     column = "delivery_date",     when_column = "shipping",     when_value = "Pickup",     readOnly = TRUE   ),   list(     column = "shipping_cost",     when_column = "shipping",     when_value = "Pickup",     readOnly = TRUE   ) )  vapply(shipping_conditions, `[[`, character(1), "column") #> [1] "address"       "delivery_date" "shipping_cost"

title: Conditional Cell Properties
code: trial_data <- data.frame(   arm = c("Control", "Treatment A"),   arm_type = c("Placebo", "Active"),   drug = c("", "Drug X"),   dose = c(0, 100),   frequency = c("", "Daily"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  trial_columns <- list(   list(name = "arm", type = "text", width = 120),   list(name = "arm_type", type = "dropdown",        source = c("Placebo", "Active"), width = 100),   list(name = "drug", type = "text", width = 120),   list(name = "dose", type = "numeric", width = 80),   list(name = "frequency", type = "dropdown",        source = c("Daily", "Twice Daily", "Weekly"), width = 100) )  trial_conditions <- list(   # Placebo arms don't need drug info   list(column = "drug", when_column = "arm_type",        when_value = "Placebo", readOnly = TRUE),   list(column = "dose", when_column = "arm_type",        when_value = "Placebo", readOnly = TRUE),   list(column = "frequency", when_column = "arm_type",        when_value = "Placebo", readOnly = TRUE) )  trial_ui <- esq_tableInput(   "trial_config",   data = trial_data,   columns = trial_columns,   cell_conditions = trial_conditions )  inherits(trial_ui, "shiny.tag") #> [1] FALSE

title: Conditional Cell Properties
text: cell value changes, conditions evaluated when_column contains when_value, target column modified Currently, readOnly = TRUE supported Disabled cells appear grayed edited Conditions evaluated per-row (row )

title: Conditional Cell Properties
text: Keep conditions simple - One value per condition Use clear trigger values - Distinct, unambiguous options Consider defaults - initial state ? Document logic - Add comments explaining rules
code: documented_conditions <- list(   # Business rule: Observed data uses datasets, not scenarios   list(     column = "scenario",     when_column = "dataType",     when_value = "Observed",     readOnly = TRUE   ) )  documented_conditions[[1]]$column #> [1] "scenario"

title: Conditional Cell Properties
text: Single value matching (ranges patterns) readOnly = TRUE property supported -row evaluation Conditions evaluated change

title: Dynamic Updates
text: updateEsqTable() lets change table’s data, dropdown/multiselect options, column configuration runtime — without recreating widget.

title: Dynamic Updates
text: function signature, arguments optional except session inputId: Pass need change: data - replacement data frame rows options - named list mapping column name new option vector columns - updated column configurations (format esq_tableInput())
code: args(updateEsqTable) #> function (session, inputId, data = NULL, options = NULL, columns = NULL)  #> NULL

title: Dynamic Updates
text: Build sales table whose country dropdown can swapped per region: Hook shiny app:
code: sales_data <- data.frame(   product = c("Widget", "Gadget"),   country = c("USA", "Canada"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  sales_columns <- list(   list(name = "product", type = "text"),   list(name = "country", type = "dropdown",        source = c("USA", "Canada", "Mexico")) )  region_choices <- list(   "North America" = c("USA", "Canada", "Mexico"),   "Europe"        = c("UK", "Germany", "France", "Spain"),   "Asia"          = c("Japan", "China", "South Korea", "India") )  region_choices[["Europe"]] #> [1] "UK"      "Germany" "France"  "Spain" ui <- fluidPage(   selectInput("region", "Region:", choices = names(region_choices)),   actionButton("update", "Update Countries"),   esq_tableInput("sales", data = sales_data, columns = sales_columns) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$update, {     updateEsqTable(session, "sales",       options = list(country = region_choices[[input$region]])     )   }) }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Dynamic Updates
text: Pass new data frame swap rows:
code: observeEvent(input$reset, {   updateEsqTable(session, "sales", data = original_data) })  # Filter and push a subset observeEvent(input$region, {   filtered <- all_data[all_data$region == input$region, ]   updateEsqTable(session, "sales", data = filtered) })

title: Dynamic Updates
text: single call can update several option vectors :
code: batched_options <- list(   country = c("USA", "Canada"),   state   = c("California", "Texas"),   city    = c("Los Angeles", "Houston") )  names(batched_options) #> [1] "country" "state"   "city" updateEsqTable(session, "my_table", options = batched_options)

title: Dynamic Updates
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   available_options <- reactive({     # Could be a database query     switch(input$category,       "Electronics" = c("Phones", "Laptops", "Tablets"),       "Clothing"    = c("Shirts", "Pants", "Shoes"),       c("Other")     )   })    observe({     updateEsqTable(session, "product_table",       options = list(subcategory = available_options())     )   }) }

title: Dynamic Updates
text: lookup table maps country list states:
code: location_data <- list(   "USA"    = c("California", "Texas", "New York"),   "Canada" = c("Ontario", "Quebec", "BC") )  location_data[["USA"]] #> [1] "California" "Texas"      "New York" server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$country, {     updateEsqTable(session, "location_table",       options = list(state = location_data[[input$country]])     )   }) }

title: Dynamic Updates
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$refresh, {     # Query from database     # categories <- dbGetQuery(con,     #   "SELECT DISTINCT category FROM products")      categories <- c("New Category 1", "New Category 2")      updateEsqTable(session, "product_table",       options = list(category = categories)     )      showNotification("Options updated!", type = "message")   }) }

title: Dynamic Updates
text: using modules, session automatically namespaced:
code: tableModuleServer <- function(id) {   moduleServer(id, function(input, output, session) {     observeEvent(input$refresh, {       # Use inputId without namespace prefix       updateEsqTable(session, "data_table",         options = list(category = new_categories)       )     })   }) }

title: Dynamic Updates
text: Option updates merge cumulatively client, either form works. single call avoids redundant round-trips preferred.
code: # Preferred: single call updateEsqTable(session, "table",   options = list(     col1 = opts1,     col2 = opts2,     col3 = opts3   ) )  # Also fine: separate calls (previous options are retained) updateEsqTable(session, "table", options = list(col1 = opts1)) updateEsqTable(session, "table", options = list(col2 = opts2))

title: Dynamic Updates
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   search_debounced <- debounce(reactive(input$search), 300)    observe({     term <- search_debounced()     options <- fetch_options(term)     updateEsqTable(session, "table",       options = list(items = options)     )   }) }

title: Dynamic Updates
text: Update needed - Don’t update every keystroke Batch updates - Combine multiple columns one call Provide feedback - Show notifications slow updates Handle invalid values - Warn users orphaned values Test edge cases - Empty options, special characters

title: Getting Started with esq.handsontable
text: esq.handsontable R shiny package provides interactive, Excel-like data table component. Built Handsontable React, offers features like dropdowns, multi-select, validation, conditional cell disabling, .

title: Getting Started with esq.handsontable
code: # Install from GitHub devtools::install_github("esqlabs/esq.handsontable")

title: Getting Started with esq.handsontable
text: Build small data set matching column configuration: esq_tableInput() returns shiny UI element. can construct outside app inspect structure: Wrap shiny app use interactively. shinyApp() call starts vignette run interactively:
code: library(shiny) library(esq.handsontable)  people <- data.frame(   name = c("Alice", "Bob", "Charlie"),   age = c(25, 30, 35),   active = c(TRUE, FALSE, TRUE),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  people_columns <- list(   list(name = "name", type = "text"),   list(name = "age", type = "numeric"),   list(name = "active", type = "checkbox") )  people #>      name age active #> 1   Alice  25   TRUE #> 2     Bob  30  FALSE #> 3 Charlie  35   TRUE table_ui <- esq_tableInput(   "my_table",   data = people,   columns = people_columns )  class(table_ui) #> [1] "shiny.tag.list" "list" ui <- fluidPage(table_ui)  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$my_table_edited, {     data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(input$my_table_edited)     print(data)   }) }  if (interactive()) {   shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Getting Started with esq.handsontable
text: Right-click cell access: insert row /, remove row, undo/redo, copy, clear selection, clear column. context_menu accepts: TRUE (default) — show items FALSE — disable menu character vector — show listed items, order. Use "---" insert separator. Valid item names: row_above, row_below, remove_row, undo, redo, copy, clear, clear_column.
code: custom_menu_ui <- esq_tableInput(   "my_table",   data = people,   columns = people_columns,   context_menu = c("row_above", "row_below", "---", "remove_row", "undo", "redo") )  inherits(custom_menu_ui, "shiny.tag") #> [1] FALSE

title: Getting Started with esq.handsontable
text: shiny server, parse JSON sent edit:
code: observeEvent(input$my_table_edited, {   data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(input$my_table_edited)   # Use the data... })

title: Getting Started with esq.handsontable
text: See vignette("column-types") detailed column configuration See vignette("conditional-cells") dynamic cell behavior See vignette("dynamic-updates") updating options runtime Check examples/ folder complete working apps

what: Interactive Data Tables with Handsontable for shiny
title: Interactive Data Tables with Handsontable for shiny
text: powerful, reusable Handsontable wrapper R shiny applications. package provides interactive data tables dropdowns, multi-select, validation, conditional cell disabling, .

title: Interactive Data Tables with Handsontable for shiny
text: Text columns - Standard text input Numeric columns - Number input validation Checkbox columns - Boolean checkbox cells Dropdown columns - Single-select dropdown validation Multi-select columns - Multi-select dropdown optional sorting Date columns - Calendar picker configurable format Conditional cell disabling - Disable cells based cell values Validation - Visual feedback dropdown values invalid Context menu - Right-click insert/remove row, undo/redo, copy, clear (items customizable per table) Action buttons - Add/delete buttons row Column tooltips - Helpful descriptions column headers

title: Interactive Data Tables with Handsontable for shiny
code: # Install from GitHub using pak (recommended) pak::pak("esqLABS/esq.handsontable")  # Or using remotes remotes::install_github("esqLABS/esq.handsontable")

title: Interactive Data Tables with Handsontable for shiny
code: library(shiny) library(esq.handsontable)  ui <- fluidPage(   esq_tableInput("myTable",     data = data.frame(       name = c("Item 1", "Item 2"),       category = c("A", "B"),       active = c(TRUE, FALSE)     ),     columns = list(       list(name = "name", type = "text"),       list(name = "category", type = "dropdown", source = c("A", "B", "C")),       list(name = "active", type = "checkbox")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$myTable_edited, {     data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(input$myTable_edited)     print(data)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server)

title: Interactive Data Tables with Handsontable for shiny
text: column configured list following options:

title: Interactive Data Tables with Handsontable for shiny
code: list(   name = "tags",   type = "multiselect",   source = c("Tag1", "Tag2", "Tag3"),   sortable = TRUE )

title: Interactive Data Tables with Handsontable for shiny
text: Use cell_conditions dynamically change cell properties based cells:
code: esq_tableInput("myTable",   data = myData,   columns = list(     list(name = "dataType", type = "dropdown", source = c("Simulated", "Observed")),     list(name = "scenario", type = "dropdown", source = scenarios),     list(name = "dataSet", type = "dropdown", source = datasets)   ),   cell_conditions = list(     # Disable scenario when dataType is "Observed"     list(       column = "scenario",       when_column = "dataType",       when_value = "Observed",       readOnly = TRUE     ),     # Disable dataSet when dataType is "Simulated"     list(       column = "dataSet",       when_column = "dataType",       when_value = "Simulated",       readOnly = TRUE     )   ) )

title: Interactive Data Tables with Handsontable for shiny
text: Add helpful tooltips column headers:
code: esq_tableInput("myTable",   data = myData,   columns = columns,   column_descriptions = list(     name = "The item's display name",     category = "Category for grouping items",     active = "Whether the item is currently active"   ) )

title: Interactive Data Tables with Handsontable for shiny
text: Use updateEsqTable update dropdown options runtime:
code: server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$refreshCategories, {     updateEsqTable(session, "myTable",       options = list(         category = c("New A", "New B", "New C")       )     )   }) }

title: esq.handsontable: Interactive Data Tables with 'Handsontable' for 'shiny' — esq.handsontable-package
text: Provides interactive, Excel-like data table input 'shiny' applications. Wraps 'Handsontable' https://handsontable.com/ 'React' offer editable tables dropdown columns, multi-select sorting, validation, conditional cell disabling, context menu, dynamic option updates runtime.

title: esq.handsontable: Interactive Data Tables with 'Handsontable' for 'shiny' — esq.handsontable-package
text: Maintainer: Anastasia Kostiv anastasiia.kostiv.ext@esqlabs.com

title: Create an esq.handsontable widget — esq_tableInput
text: Creates interactive, Excel-like data table using Handsontable. Supports text, numeric, checkbox, dropdown, multi-select, date columns features like validation, conditional cell disabling, calendar pickers, .

title: Create an esq.handsontable widget — esq_tableInput
code: esq_tableInput(   inputId,   data = NULL,   columns = list(),   options = list(),   column_descriptions = list(),   show_action_buttons = TRUE,   context_menu = TRUE,   none_value = "--NONE--",   height = "100%",   width = "100%",   cell_conditions = list() )

title: Create an esq.handsontable widget — esq_tableInput
text: inputId input slot used access value. data data frame containing initial data display. columns list column configurations (see Details). options named list dropdown options can referenced columns. column_descriptions named list column descriptions tooltips. show_action_buttons Whether show add/delete buttons column (default: TRUE). context_menu Controls right-click context menu. Accepts: TRUE (default) - show built-items FALSE - disable menu character vector item names - show listed items, given order. Valid names: "row_above", "row_below", "remove_row", "undo", "redo", "copy", "clear", "clear_column". Use "---" insert separator. none_value Deprecated ignored. Dropdown cells can cleared Delete Backspace key. Kept backward compatibility. height Table height CSS value (default fills container). width Table width CSS value (default fills container). cell_conditions list conditional cell configurations (see Details).

title: Create an esq.handsontable widget — esq_tableInput
text: Shiny UI element containing Handsontable widget.

title: Create an esq.handsontable widget — esq_tableInput
text: column list following elements: name - Column name (required) type - Column type: "text", "numeric", "checkbox", "dropdown", "multiselect", "date" (required) source - Options dropdown/multiselect columns sortable - Enable drag--drop sorting multiselect (default: FALSE) validate - Validate dropdown values (default: TRUE) readOnly - Make column read-(default: FALSE) width - Column width pixels dateFormat - Moment.js format string date columns (default: "YYYY-MM-DD") correctFormat - Auto-correct user-typed dates declared format (default: TRUE) defaultDate - Date shown cell empty calendar picker opens

title: Create an esq.handsontable widget — esq_tableInput
text: built-right-click menu includes: insert row /, remove row, undo/redo, copy, clear selection, clear column. Set context_menu = FALSE disable , pass character vector item names show subset - example context_menu = c("row_above", "row_below", "---", "remove_row").

title: Create an esq.handsontable widget — esq_tableInput
text: Cell conditions allow dynamic cell properties based cell values. condition list : column - column apply condition when_column - column check when_value - value triggers condition readOnly - Set cell read-condition met type - Change cell type condition met source - Change dropdown options condition met

title: Create an esq.handsontable widget — esq_tableInput
code: if (interactive()) { library(shiny)  ui <- fluidPage(   esq_tableInput("myTable",     data = data.frame(       name = c("Item 1", "Item 2"),       category = c("A", "B"),       active = c(TRUE, FALSE),       stringsAsFactors = FALSE     ),     columns = list(       list(name = "name", type = "text"),       list(name = "category", type = "dropdown", source = c("A", "B", "C")),       list(name = "active", type = "checkbox")     )   ) )  server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$myTable_edited, {     data <- jsonlite::fromJSON(input$myTable_edited)     print(data)   }) }  shinyApp(ui, server) }

title: Update an esq.handsontable widget — updateEsqTable
text: Dynamically updates data, dropdown options, column configurations existing esq.handsontable widget without recreating table.

title: Update an esq.handsontable widget — updateEsqTable
code: updateEsqTable(session, inputId, data = NULL, options = NULL, columns = NULL)

title: Update an esq.handsontable widget — updateEsqTable
text: session Shiny session object. inputId input ID table update. data New data frame (optional). options named list names column names values character vectors new dropdown options (optional). columns Updated column configurations (optional).

title: Update an esq.handsontable widget — updateEsqTable
text: Called side effect (sends message client). Returns invisible(NULL).

title: Update an esq.handsontable widget — updateEsqTable
code: if (interactive()) { server <- function(input, output, session) {   observeEvent(input$update_button, {     updateEsqTable(session, "my_table",       options = list(         category = c("New A", "New B", "New C"),         status = c("Active", "Inactive")       )     )   }) } }

title: esq.handsontable 1.0.0
text: esq_tableInput() - Create interactive data tables Shiny updateEsqTable() - Dynamically update data, dropdown options, column configuration runtime

title: esq.handsontable 1.0.0
text: text - Standard text input numeric - Number input validation checkbox - Boolean checkbox cells dropdown - Single-select dropdown validation multiselect - Multi-select dropdown optional drag--drop sorting date - Calendar picker (Pikaday); configurable via dateFormat (default "YYYY-MM-DD"), correctFormat, defaultDate

title: esq.handsontable 1.0.0
text: Conditional cell properties - disable re-type cells based cell values Column tooltips - helpful descriptions column headers Validation - visual feedback dropdown values fall outside source Customizable right-click context menu - insert row /, remove row, undo/redo, copy, clear selection, clear column. context_menu accepts TRUE, FALSE, character vector item names fine-grained control (use "---" separators) Action buttons column - add/delete buttons row Cumulative option updates - calling updateEsqTable(options = ...) merges previously sent options rather replacing , per-column updates can issued independently
